bodyHtml:
Home/FHA Condo Loans Florida
FHA Condo Loans Florida

FHA condo loans in Florida

Condos can be financed with FHA in Florida — but the building must be FHA-approved. Here's how project and single-unit approval work, and how to check before you buy.

Condos

FHA condo loans in Florida

Florida is a condo state — and condos can absolutely be financed with an FHA loan, but there's an extra step: the condo has to be FHA-approved, either at the project level or through single-unit approval.

Project approval vs. single-unit approval

If the entire condo project is on HUD's FHA-approved list, any qualifying unit can be financed with FHA. If it's not, FHA's Single-Unit Approval (SUA) process may let you finance an individual unit in an otherwise non-approved building that meets certain conditions.

Why it matters in Florida

From Brickell and Edgewater to beachfront communities, much of Florida's affordable inventory is condos. Checking FHA approval before you fall in love with a unit saves time and heartbreak — a non-approved building can stop an FHA deal cold.

What we do

We verify a building's FHA status up front and guide you toward eligible units, or pursue single-unit approval where it makes sense — so you can shop condos with confidence.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

Can I buy a condo with an FHA loan in Florida?+
Yes, as long as the condo project is FHA-approved or the unit qualifies through FHA's Single-Unit Approval process.
How do I know if a condo is FHA-approved?+
You can check HUD's FHA-approved condo list, or we can verify a specific building's status for you before you make an offer.
What is FHA Single-Unit Approval (SUA)?+
SUA lets you finance an individual unit in a building that isn't fully FHA-approved, provided the unit and project meet certain requirements.
Why are some Florida condos not FHA-approved?+
Approval depends on factors like owner-occupancy ratios, the project's finances and reserves, insurance, and litigation status. Buildings must meet HUD's criteria.
